title: Maintenance Check
description: A scheduled execution of a maintenance plan at a site. The unit of work a technician is assigned to. Carries the schedule state (status, due_date, started, completed) and an optional frequency override. Source codes (Delta WO, Maximo) often encode the frequency as a suffix on the plan code (e.g. 'LVACB-A' = LVACB plan, A=annual frequency) — the suffix should be parsed off and stored here on the check.

external_id.description: External system reference. For Maximo, use maximo_wo_number instead.

plan_id.description: Maintenance plan this check executes. Optional — ad-hoc checks may have no plan.

assigned_to_user_id.description: Technician this check is assigned to. Resolves against staff via the auth user mapping.

status.description: Workflow state. 'overdue' is derived (due_date < today AND status='scheduled'); imports may pre-set it.

due_date.description: When the check is required to be performed by.

start_date.description: Planned start date (window opens). Distinct from started_at which is the actual start timestamp.

started_at.description: Actual timestamp the technician started work.

completed_at.description: Actual timestamp the check was closed.

frequency.description: Frequency for this specific check, often parsed from the plan code suffix. Delta/Maximo encode suffixes A/Q/M/S/W/2/3/5/8/10 (e.g. 'LVACB-A' → frequency=annual). Falls back to maintenance_plan.frequency if not set.

is_dark_site.description: Whether this check is a dark-site (no live electrical work) visit.

custom_name.description: Override display name for this specific check (defaults to plan name if null).

maximo_wo_number.description: Maximo work order number. Often the most stable cross-system ID.

maximo_pm_number.description: Maximo PM number (the recurring schedule that generated the WO).

x-eq-cross-field-rules.rule: completed_at == null OR started_at == null OR completed_at >= started_at

x-eq-cross-field-rules.message: Status is 'complete' but completed_at is null.
Frequency could be derived from the plan code suffix (e.g. 'LVACB-A' → annual). Importer should parse the trailing suffix off the plan_code and set frequency here.
x-eq-cross-field-rules.hint: Parse trailing -X off the plan code: A=annual, Q=quarterly, M=monthly, S=semi_annual, W=weekly, 2=2yr, 3=quarterly, 5=5yr, 6=semi_annual, 8=8yr, 10=10yr. See lib/import/delta-wo-parser.ts FREQUENCY_SUFFIX_MAP in eq-solves-service for the canonical map.

x-eq-import-hints.description: Maintenance checks usually come from a Delta work-order export or a Maximo WO sheet, with one row per (asset, visit). The plan code carries the frequency as a suffix.

common_sources.name: Delta work-order export
watchouts: Job Plan column is 'CODE-SUFFIX' format — split to plan_id resolution + frequency
Status values are Maximo-coded (WAPPROV, WSCHED, etc.) — see x-eq-enum-aliases

common_sources.name: Manual schedule spreadsheet
watchouts: No WO number — leave maximo_wo_number null
